2026-04-17T09:04:11.9622321Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9622899Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9623355Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9623725Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9624036Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9624271Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9624434Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9624612Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9624781Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9625010Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-17T09:04:11.9625517Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T09:04:11.9625776Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from...

2026-04-17T09:06:48.6201336Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T09:05:20.677339676Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58275,\"title\":\"[ServiceFabric] Docs: README examples, key concepts, and CHANGELOG missing after TypeSpec migration\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58149 (commit `91e692166d`) by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58149 completed the MPG migration for `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric`, regenerating the entire package from TypeSpec. This introduced/confirmed all core resource types (`ServiceFabricClusterResource`, `ServiceFabricApplicationResource`, `ServiceFabricApplicationTypeResource`, `ServiceFabricApplicationTypeVersionResource`, `ServiceFabricServiceResource`, `ServiceFabricVmSizeResource`) and their associated collections and extension methods.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG is empty for 1.2.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)**: The TypeSpec migration should be documented — at minimum noting the regeneration and any behavioral changes or new API surface introduced by the 2023-11-01-preview API version.\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is generic**: It only links to the general Azure SDK docs page. There are no ServiceFabric-specific key concepts (clusters, node types, applications, application types, services, reliability/durability levels, upgrade policies, etc.).\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section has no inline code snippets**: It only links to a general samples gallery. There are no embedded code examples for the most common operations (create a cluster, list clusters by subscription, create an application, update a cluster).\\n- **No handwritten sample files with `#region Snippet:` tags**: Generated samples exist in `tests/Generated/Samples/` but lack `#region Snippet:` markers and are not wired to README placeholders, so the Update-Snippets tooling cannot inject them into the README.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Update the CHANGELOG\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — populate the `1.2.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section.\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Migrated SDK generation from AutoRest to TypeSpec.\\n- Regenerated from the `2023-11-01-preview` API version specification.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 2: Update README Key Concepts section\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the current \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section body with ServiceFabric-specific concepts.\\n\\nReplace:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric` library provides management-plane access to Azure Service Fabric resources. The key resource hierarchy is:\\n\\n- **`ServiceFabricClusterResource`** — represents a Service Fabric cluster; this is the top-level resource managed by this library. Clusters define the node types, reliability level, and durability level for the distributed system.\\n- **`ServiceFabricApplicationTypeResource`** — represents a named application type registered on the cluster.\\n- **`ServiceFabricApplicationTypeVersionResource`** — represents a specific version of an application type, including the package URL.\\n- **`ServiceFabricApplicationResource`** — represents a deployed instance of an application type on a cluster.\\n- **`ServiceFabricServiceResource`** — represents a service within a deployed application.\\n- **`ServiceFabricVmSizeResource`** — read-only resource exposing the supported VM sizes for Service Fabric node types in a given location.\\n\\nFor additional general concepts about the Azure Resource Manager .NET SDK, see the [Azure SDK for .NET documentation]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 3: Add handwritten sample file with snippet regions\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/tests/Samples/Sample_ServiceFabricCluster.cs` *(create)*\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_ServiceFabricCluster\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateOrUpdateCluster()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateServiceFabricCluster\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n\\n            ServiceFabricClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroupResource.GetServiceFabricClusters();\\n\\n            ServiceFabricClusterData data = new ServiceFabricClusterData(new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                ManagementEndpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(mycluster.eastus.cloudapp.azure.com/redacted)\\u0026#34;),\\n                NodeTypes =\\n                {\\n                    new ClusterNodeTypeDescription(\\u0026#34;nt1vm\\u0026#34;, 19000, 19007, true, 5)\\n                    {\\n                        ApplicationPorts = new ClusterEndpointRangeDescription(20000, 30000),\\n                        EphemeralPorts = new ClusterEndpointRangeDescription(49000, 64000),\\n                        DurabilityLevel = ClusterDurabilityLevel.Silver,\\n                    }\\n                },\\n                ReliabilityLevel = ClusterReliabilityLevel.Silver,\\n                FabricSettings =\\n                {\\n                    new SettingsSectionDescription(\\u0026#34;Security\\u0026#34;, new[]\\n                    {\\n                        new SettingsParameterDescription(\\u0026#34;ClusterProtectionLevel\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;EncryptAndSign\\u0026#34;)\\n                    })\\n                },\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro = await clusters.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myCluster\\u0026#34;, data);\\n            ServiceFabricClusterResource cluster = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Cluster ID: {cluster.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListClustersBySubscription()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListServiceFabricClusters\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            await foreach (ServiceFabricClusterResource cluster in subscription.GetServiceFabricClustersAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Cluster: {cluster.Data.Name}, Location: {cluster.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 4: Update README Examples section with snippet placeholders\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — in a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor additional code samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** The closing fences above are The key resource hierarchy is:\\n\\n- **`ServiceFabricClusterResource`** — represents a Service Fabric cluster; this is the top-level resource managed by this library. Clusters define the node types, reliability level, and durability level for the distributed system.\\n- **`ServiceFabricApplicationTypeResource`** — represents a named application type registered on the cluster.\\n- **`ServiceFabricApplicationTypeVersionResource`** — represents a specific version of an application type, including the package URL.\\n- **`ServiceFabricApplicationResource`** — represents a deployed instance of an application type on a cluster.\\n- **`ServiceFabricServiceResource`** — represents a service within a deployed application.\\n- **`ServiceFabricVmSizeResource`** — read-only resource exposing the supported VM sizes for Service Fabric node types in a given location.\\n\\nFor additional general concepts about the Azure Resource Manager .NET SDK, see the [Azure SDK for .NET documentation]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 3: Add handwritten sample file with snippet regions\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/tests/Samples/Sample_ServiceFabricCluster.cs` *(create)*\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateOrUpdateCluster()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateServiceFabricCluster\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n\\n            ServiceFabricClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroupResource.GetServiceFabricClusters();\\n\\n            ServiceFabricClusterData data = new ServiceFabricClusterData(new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                ManagementEndpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;(mycluster.eastus.cloudapp.azure.com/redacted)\\u0026#34;),\\n                NodeTypes =\\n                {\\n                    new ClusterNodeTypeDescription(\\u0026#34;nt1vm\\u0026#34;, 19000, 19007, true, 5)\\n                    {\\n                        ApplicationPorts = new ClusterEndpointRangeDescription(20000, 30000),\\n                        EphemeralPorts = new ClusterEndpointRangeDescription(49000, 64000),\\n                        DurabilityLevel = ClusterDurabilityLevel.Silver,\\n                    }\\n                },\\n                ReliabilityLevel = ClusterReliabilityLevel.Silver,\\n                FabricSettings =\\n                {\\n                    new SettingsSectionDescription(\\u0026#34;Security\\u0026#34;, new[]\\n                    {\\n                        new SettingsParameterDescription(\\u0026#34;ClusterProtectionLevel\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;EncryptAndSign\\u0026#34;)\\n                    })\\n                },\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro = await clusters.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myCluster\\u0026#34;, data);\\n            ServiceFabricClusterResource cluster = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Cluster ID: {cluster.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListClustersBySubscription()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListServiceFabricClusters\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            await foreach (ServiceFabricClusterResource cluster in subscription.GetServiceFabricClustersAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Cluster: {cluster.Data.Name}, Location: {cluster.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n
